accept: A bundle_strategy config knob exists with values per_op (current behavior) and per_issue (new default).

On a per_issue repo, a typical 'create issue (separately), claim, work, close' sequence produces 1 act-op commit per issue lifecycle (or 0 if claim/close happen as part of the work commit; the design doc clarifies).
--no-commit continues to work as today on both per_op and per_issue repos.
Rollback test: simulate a close that fails mid-commit; assert no dangling staged op files.
Concurrency test: two agents running on different issues in the same working tree complete cleanly.
act doctor's orphan-close check still passes on a repo that uses per_issue bundling.
act log <id> output is unchanged regardless of bundle_strategy.
No history rewriting: existing pre-bundling commits are untouched on conversion.
payload.description:
Per docs/commit-noise-design.md (act-6018), implement bundling so a typical 'work an issue end-to-end' loop produces ~1 git commit instead of ~4-5. Reduces 69% of current commits-since-v0.1.0 (the act-op bookkeeping noise) toward zero.

**Design decision (orchestrator call, 2026-05-10):** auto-bundle on close; preserve existing --no-commit flag for explicit batching. Reasoning: keeps the canonical loop unchanged for agents (no new flush primitive to remember), and --no-commit already covers the explicit-batch use case (bootstrap, migrations). Tradeoff: the close commit becomes a bigger atomic unit; mid-close partial-batch failures must roll back cleanly.

**What this means in practice:**
- Default behavior changes: claim, dep add, update, etc. write op files but DO NOT auto-commit when an open claim exists for the issue (i.e. when we're between claim and close on the same issue, hold the commit).
- close auto-commits the entire pending batch for that issue (claim op + any intermediate ops + close op) in a single commit.
- --no-commit preserved as today: agents can still write ops without commits and bundle manually for cross-issue batching.
- Behind a config knob (e.g. .act/config.json bundle_strategy: per_op | per_issue) so existing repos don't break; default = per_issue going forward, but per_op stays available.

**Things the implementation must get right:**
- Rollback semantics: if any op in the pending batch fails or its close is aborted, the partial batch unstages cleanly (don't leave dangling staged ops).
- Concurrency: two agents working different issues in the same tree must not stomp on each other's pending batches. The git index serialization concern remains but is bounded to close-time, not per-op.
- act doctor: must still recognise the (act-XXXX) markers in bundled commits — likely a no-op since the marker still appears in the close commit subject.
- Existing v0.1.0 commits in this repo's history have the per-op format and must not break doctor or log filtering. No history rewriting.
- act log <id> must still reconstruct the per-op timeline from the .act/ ops directory (the source of truth) regardless of git-commit grouping.
- Migration: docs/commit-noise-design.md should call out that switching an existing repo from per_op to per_issue is safe — past commits stay; only future ops bundle.
payload.title: implement bundling: auto-bundle act-op commits into one commit per issue (close)
